name: Veracruz (Mexico)

colors.symbolism: the field and details of the arms

description: A white field with the coat of arms of the state of Veracruz centred.
history: Veracruz has no officially adopted state flag; the design shown — a white field bearing the state coat of arms — is in de facto use to represent the state.

features.symbolism: A white field charged with the state coat of arms, in the typical pattern of Mexican state flags.

The arms reference the founding of the Villa Rica de la Vera Cruz by Hernán Cortés in 1519 — the first Spanish settlement on the American mainland — together with allusions to the Gulf coast and to the title 'Heroica' awarded to the city.
